summary: Aunt Pythia, Cathy O'Neil's advice column persona, defers her usual column to highlight an exceptional Ask Polly advice piece about self-worth and relationships. The column so impressed her that she considers it the best advice she's ever seen, applicable to both men and women. Rather than answering her own questions, she urges readers to read Polly's response about stopping the search for external validation.
key_points: The post is a rare instance of Aunt Pythia ceding her column to recommend another advice writer
The Ask Polly piece addresses why someone feels unloved by romantic partners
The advice centers on rejecting external validation and embracing self-worth
O'Neil finds the advice universally applicable regardless of gender
money_quotes: Is it time to stop being so good and start discovering what's going to transform your life into something big and vibrant and shocking?
Block the 'other' from this picture. No more audience. You are the cherished and the cherisher.
Fuck wondering if you're lovable. Fuck asking someone else, 'Am I there yet?' Fuck listening for the answer.
key_insight: Sometimes the best advice is to stop seeking external validation and become your own source of worth and love.
